Summerlike Sunday, Then Cooler

Last Updated Sun March 22 at 10:50AM by Alan Simmons

Increasing sunshine and warm temperatures today have us feeling more like June instead of late-March, but your summer preview ends tonight. A front comes through early Monday with breezy winds, a few showers, and much cooler temperatures to start the new work week, but still warmer than seasonal averages for early Spring.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Forecast Discussion

Summerlike temperatures today are thanks to high pressure over the Gulf of Mexico, which is pulling very warm air over the Ohio and Tennessee Valleys our way. Wouldn't be surprised at all to see a few lower-elevation spots hit the 80-degree mark. In the meantime, a cold front over the lower Great Lakes region this morning will push southward. The front will reach our area early Monday morning, bringing clouds and some showers (especially along the western escarpment), along with gusty winds and much cooler temperatures.

Quiet weather returns for Tuesday and Wednesday as high pressure tracks across the lower Great Lakes region. Tuesday is the coolest with temperatures near seasonal averages, but after that, temperatures moderate day-by-day through the rest of the work week. Fast westerly flow aloft makes timing disturbances a little troublesome. There could be a few showers Wed night into Thursday, but stay tuned, as the timing could change. Another front comes in from the north about Friday with more showers and another shot of cooler air for next weekend.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for March 16, 2026

Quiet weather returns for western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia after a hyper-active Monday around the region. Severe weather is followed by a sharp cold shot and mountain snow shower episode, then we moderate nicely for late week through the weekend.

Mid-March Madness

A strong cold front crosses the Southern Appalachians Monday morning and moves into the Foothills Monday afternoon.

Mar 16, 2026
Severe Weather Season Around The Corner

As our March weather has become more changeable and volatile, it's a reminder that severe weather season is almost upon us.

Last week was Severe Weather Awareness Week in North Carolina; This week is Virginia's turn.

While western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia may have less overall severe weather than our surrounding neighbors, we still have our fair share, and it's a great time to review your severe weather emergency/preparedeness procedures ahead of time.
